description: Goal: remove the remaining places where any or none encode uncertainty, missing evidence, or incomplete convergence rather than real source/spec semantics, after activation partition keys and ReturnEvidence have made those states representable directly. Signal: compiler2 semantic/type tests that previously depended on fake top/bottom returns pass with explicit evidence states; telemetry shows current vs settled evidence movement and partition-key convergence instead of fake any/none facts. Strategy: preserve the failing assertions as acceptance tests, trace each remaining any/none to the earliest wrong fact, and repair the data model rather than downstream display, artifact, or planner code. Non-goal: do not solve activation overspecialization here; that belongs to fz-rh2.18.9. Outro: no missing-return-as-none, no unresolved-as-any, no underconstrained-call-as-any, and no local fallback shim that hides unknown evidence inside an ordinary domain type.
title: Compiler2 type convergence: burn down any/none poison fallbacks
